---
description: "Oliver Frings — PhD candidate in economics at AgroParisTech and the Climate Economics Chair, Paris. Carbon market integrity and public preferences for climate and forest policy."
title: "Oliver Frings"
---

<!-- TODO: when a headshot exists, add it as assets/profile.jpg and place
     <img src="assets/profile.jpg" class="hero-photo"> floated right of the intro. -->

[PhD candidate in Economics · AgroParisTech & Climate Economics Chair, Paris]{.lead-line}

## About

[I study whether nature markets deliver what they promise. On the demand side, I measure public support for forest conservation and carbon offsetting across Europe with discrete choice experiments. On the supply side, I price what a carbon credit actually delivers — net of leakage, storage duration, and reversal risk — with actuarial models and Monte Carlo simulation.]{.lead-line}

[Email](mailto:oliver.frings@agroparistech.fr){.pill}
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=DgFqKHYAAAAJ){.pill}
[ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-2682-2304){.pill}
[GitHub](https://github.com/o-frings){.pill}
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/oliver-frings-x161/){.pill}
[CV (PDF)](assets/CV_Oliver_Frings.pdf){.pill}

## Positions

[2023–present]{.when} [PhD Candidate in Economics]{.what} [[AgroParisTech](https://www.agroparistech.fr/) · Bureau d'Économie Théorique et Appliquée (BETA), Nancy — thesis *Pricing Credibility: Supply-Side Integrity and Public Acceptability in Nature Markets*, defence expected December 2026]{.meta}

[2025–present]{.when} [Research Fellow]{.what} [[Climate Economics Chair](https://www.chaireeconomieduclimat.org/en/), Paris]{.meta}

[2025–present]{.when} [Visiting Scholar]{.what} [Department of Land Economy ([C-EENRG](https://www.ceenrg.landecon.cam.ac.uk/)), University of Cambridge]{.meta}

## Education

[2023–2026]{.when} [PhD in Economics]{.what} [AgroParisTech, Bureau d'Économie Théorique et Appliquée (BETA), Nancy & Palaiseau — expected December 2026]{.meta}

[2020–2022]{.when} [MSc in European Forestry (Erasmus Mundus)]{.what} [University of Eastern Finland & University of Lleida — thesis published in *Ecological Economics* (2023)]{.meta}

[2016–2020]{.when} [BSc in Forest Sciences]{.what} [Dresden University of Technology]{.meta}

## Awards

[2024]{.when} [Highly Commended Paper Award]{.what} [8th Workshop on Non-Market Valuation, University of Leeds]{.meta}

[2023]{.when} [Extraordinary Prize for Master's Degrees]{.what} [University of Lleida — best academic record 2021/22]{.meta}

## Grants

[2025]{.when} [FOCUSED Seed Project Grant]{.what} [AAP ARTEMIS — €15,000 project grant, €2,000 allocated to survey expansion]{.meta}

[2024]{.when} [FairCarboN Research Exchange Grant]{.what} [French National Research Agency — €7,000, research exchange to the University of Cambridge]{.meta}

[2024]{.when} [DrEAM Research Exchange Grant]{.what} [University of Lorraine — €5,300, research exchange to the University of Cambridge]{.meta}

[2024]{.when} [ABIES Research Exchange Grant]{.what} [AgroParisTech — €1,000, research exchange to the University of Cambridge]{.meta}

[2024–2026]{.when} [Agreenium (EIR-A) — DESSE International Mobility Grant]{.what} [INRAE / Agreenium — €2,000, research exchange to the University of Cambridge]{.meta}

[2022]{.when} [ORION Master's Grant]{.what} [French National Research Agency — €7,000, research scholarship for excellent foreign students]{.meta}

[2021]{.when} [ETSEA Grant Programme]{.what} [University of Lleida — €2,500, Erasmus Mundus European Forestry scholarship]{.meta}
